input.background: This fictitious but realistic example considers odds ratios from a case-control study on asbestos and lung cancer while considering the concurrent effects of smoking.
input.question: Combining these heterogeneous odds ratios to form a single summary odds ratio would not adequately predict the joint effects of smoking and asbestos on lung cancer risk.

output.method: 分类或发生概率影响因素解读
output.answer: Therefore, a statistical interaction is said to exist.

asbestos.value: Presence or absence of asbestos exposure
asbestos.description: Indicates whether an individual has been exposed to asbestos.

smoking.description: Indicates the smoking status of an individual.

lung_cancer.value: Presence or absence of lung cancer
lung_cancer.description: Indicates whether an individual has been diagnosed with lung cancer.

odds_ratios.value: Odds ratios from the case-control study
odds_ratios.description: Odds ratios calculated from the case-control study for different groups.

meta_info.caption: Statistical interaction (Asbestos, cigarettes, and lung cancer).
